user_query:
Hi! I’m planning my pirate allowance and want to compare my toy spending to famous shipwreck treasure. Please use the files in input/ to build three things for me, saving the results into output/:

1) Extract shipwreck treasure estimates from input/shipwreck_notes.html (the table on that page) and convert them to USD using the exchange rates in input/pirate_budget_config.yaml. Then apply the salvage fee defined as SALVAGE_FEE_RATE in input/treasure_reader.py to compute a net value. Save a CSV to output/shipwreck_loot.csv with columns: shipwreck, year, raw_amount, raw_unit, usd_value, usd_after_salvage. All USD numbers should be rounded to 2 decimals.

2) Look through the input/expenses/ directory for every purchases_*.csv file, combine them by month (month is the YYYY-MM part in the filename), sum total_spent_usd per category for each month, and compare each sum to the matching budget cap from input/pirate_budget_config.yaml (budget_caps_usd). Save a CSV to output/monthly_budget_summary.csv with columns: month, category, total_spent_usd, budget_cap_usd, over_budget (true/false). Round USD to 2 decimals.

3) Convert my piggy bank contents from input/piggy_bank.json to USD using the exchange rates in input/pirate_budget_config.yaml and produce output/piggy_bank_usd.json with keys: per_unit_usd (an object mapping each unit to its USD value) and total_usd (rounded to 2 decimals).

Important constraints:
- Do not hardcode the salvage fee or exchange rates; read SALVAGE_FEE_RATE from input/treasure_reader.py and exchange_rates from input/pirate_budget_config.yaml.
- Parse the shipwreck data by extracting the structured table in input/shipwreck_notes.html (treat unit names case-insensitively and handle commas in numbers).
- Enumerating and reading all files under input/expenses/ is required; do not assume a fixed list.
- Create the output directory if it doesn’t exist and write the files exactly at: output/shipwreck_loot.csv, output/monthly_budget_summary.csv, and output/piggy_bank_usd.json. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
